psm.name: Professional Scrum Master™ I
psm.description: Scrum Master – Rolle, Events, Empirie und Werte (Scrum Guide 2020).

questions.question: How large is a Scrum Team typically?

options.D: As many as the work requires

questions.explanation: The Scrum Guide states a Scrum Team is typically 10 or fewer people; smaller teams communicate better and are more productive.

questions.question: A Scrum Team consists of:

options.A: A Scrum Master, a Product Owner, and a Project Manager
options.B: A Scrum Master, a Product Owner, and Developers
options.C: A Scrum Master, a Product Owner, Developers, and stakeholders
options.D: A Team Lead, a Product Owner, and Developers

questions.explanation: A Scrum Team comprises one Scrum Master, one Product Owner, and Developers. There are no sub-teams or hierarchies.

questions.question: Who is accountable for maximizing the value of the product resulting from the work of the Scrum Team?
questions.explanation: The Product Owner is accountable for maximizing the value of the product resulting from the work of the Scrum Team.

questions.question: What are the three pillars of empiricism?
questions.explanation: Empiricism rests on three pillars: Transparency, Inspection, and Adaptation.

questions.question: Which set lists the five Scrum Values?
questions.explanation: The five Scrum Values are Commitment, Courage, Focus, Openness, and Respect.

questions.question: Who can cancel a Sprint?
questions.explanation: Only the Product Owner has the authority to cancel a Sprint (e.g., if the Sprint Goal becomes obsolete).

questions.question: What is the maximum length of a Sprint?
questions.explanation: Sprints are fixed-length events of one month or less.

questions.question: True or False: A new Sprint starts immediately after the conclusion of the previous Sprint.
questions.explanation: True. A new Sprint starts immediately after the conclusion of the previous one.

questions.question: The Daily Scrum is time-boxed to:
questions.explanation: The Daily Scrum is a 15-minute event for the Developers.

questions.question: For whom is the Daily Scrum?

options.B: For the Scrum Master to track progress
options.D: For the Product Owner to reassign work

questions.explanation: The Daily Scrum is for the Developers to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the Sprint Backlog.

questions.question: Which event acts as the container for all other Scrum events?
questions.explanation: The Sprint is a container for all other events (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective).

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Sprint Review?

options.A: To inspect the Increment and adapt the Product Backlog
options.D: To formally sign off the project

questions.explanation: The Sprint Review inspects the outcome of the Sprint (the Increment) and determines future adaptations to the Product Backlog. It is a working session, not a sign-off gate.

questions.question: The Sprint Retrospective focuses on:

options.B: Inspecting how the last Sprint went (people, interactions, process, tools, Definition of Done) and planning improvements

questions.explanation: The Retrospective inspects how the last Sprint went regarding individuals, interactions, processes, tools, and the Definition of Done, and identifies improvements.

questions.question: What is the time-box for Sprint Planning for a one-month Sprint?
questions.explanation: Sprint Planning is time-boxed to a maximum of 8 hours for a one-month Sprint (usually shorter for shorter Sprints).

questions.question: Sprint Planning addresses which of the following topics? (Choose all that apply.)

options.B: What can be Done this Sprint?
options.C: How will the chosen work get done?
options.D: Who will be assigned each task by the Scrum Master?

questions.explanation: Sprint Planning addresses Why (the Sprint Goal/value), What (which items), and How (the plan). Tasks are not assigned by the Scrum Master; the Developers self-organize.

questions.question: The Sprint Goal is the commitment for which artifact?
questions.explanation: The Sprint Goal is the commitment for the Sprint Backlog.

questions.question: The Product Goal is the commitment for which artifact?
questions.explanation: The Product Goal is the commitment for the Product Backlog.

questions.question: The commitment for the Increment is:
questions.explanation: The Definition of Done is the commitment for the Increment.

questions.question: If the Definition of Done is not an organizational standard, who must create it?
questions.explanation: If the Definition of Done is not an organizational standard, the Scrum Team must create one appropriate for the product.

questions.question: True or False: If a Product Backlog item does not meet the Definition of Done, it can still be released as long as the Product Owner approves.
questions.explanation: False. If an item does not meet the Definition of Done it cannot be released or even presented at the Sprint Review; it returns to the Product Backlog.

questions.question: Who is accountable for the Sprint Backlog?
questions.explanation: The Sprint Backlog is created by and belongs to the Developers.

questions.question: The Scrum Master is accountable for:

options.A: Managing the team and assigning tasks

questions.explanation: The Scrum Master is accountable for the Scrum Team's effectiveness by helping it improve its practices.

questions.question: The Scrum Master serves which of the following? (Choose all that apply.)
questions.explanation: The Scrum Master serves the Scrum Team, the Product Owner, and the wider organization.

questions.question: Which statement about self-managing Scrum Teams is correct?

options.A: The Scrum Master decides who does what, when, and how
options.B: The Scrum Team internally decides who does what, when, and how
options.C: The Product Owner assigns all tasks

questions.explanation: Self-managing means the Scrum Team internally decides who does what, when, and how.

questions.question: What is Scrum founded on?
questions.explanation: Scrum is founded on empiricism and lean thinking.

questions.question: True or False: The Scrum Master is a management role with authority over the Developers.
questions.explanation: False. The Scrum Master is a true leader who serves; the role has no command authority over the Developers.

questions.question: How much of the Product Backlog must be fully detailed before a Sprint can begin?

options.A: The entire backlog must be fully detailed
options.B: Enough to enable the start; refinement is ongoing
options.D: Only the current Sprint's items, fully estimated

questions.explanation: Only enough detail is needed to start; the Product Backlog is emergent and continuously refined.

questions.question: Product Backlog refinement is best described as:

options.B: The ongoing act of breaking down and further defining Product Backlog items into smaller, more precise items
options.C: An activity done only by the Product Owner
options.D: Something performed once at project start

questions.explanation: Refinement is the ongoing act of breaking down and further defining items so they are precise enough to be worked on.

questions.question: Who is responsible for ordering the Product Backlog?
questions.explanation: The Product Owner orders (prioritizes) the Product Backlog.

questions.question: True or False: During a Sprint, scope may be clarified and renegotiated between the Product Owner and the Developers as more is learned.
questions.explanation: True. Scope may be clarified and renegotiated with the Product Owner as more is learned, as long as the Sprint Goal remains.

questions.question: The Developers realize mid-Sprint that the selected scope is larger than they can complete, though the Sprint Goal is still achievable. What is the most appropriate action?

options.B: Negotiate the scope with the Product Owner

questions.explanation: The Developers negotiate scope with the Product Owner. The Sprint length is fixed and the Sprint is not extended.

questions.question: An Increment is best described as:

options.B: A concrete stepping stone toward the Product Goal; it must be usable
options.C: The same thing as the Sprint Backlog
options.D: Something delivered only at the end of the project

questions.explanation: An Increment is a concrete, usable stepping stone toward the Product Goal.

questions.question: How many Increments may be created within a single Sprint?
questions.explanation: Multiple Increments may be created within a single Sprint.

questions.question: True or False: The Sprint Review is merely a status presentation to stakeholders.
questions.explanation: False. The Sprint Review is a working session to inspect and adapt, not just a status presentation.

questions.question: The maximum time-box of the Sprint Retrospective for a one-month Sprint is:
questions.explanation: The Sprint Retrospective is time-boxed to a maximum of 3 hours for a one-month Sprint.

questions.question: The maximum time-box of the Sprint Review for a one-month Sprint is:
questions.explanation: The Sprint Review is time-boxed to a maximum of 4 hours for a one-month Sprint.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the Product Owner's authority?

options.A: The Product Owner only makes suggestions; the Developers decide the order
options.B: The entire organization must respect the Product Owner's decisions
options.C: The Scrum Master can override the Product Owner
options.D: Stakeholders order the backlog by vote

questions.explanation: For the Product Owner to succeed, the entire organization must respect their decisions; no one can force the Developers to work from a different set of requirements.

questions.question: A Scrum Master notices the Daily Scrum regularly runs 45 minutes and turns into whole-team problem-solving. What is the most appropriate action?

options.A: Let it continue; longer is better
options.B: Coach the Developers that the Daily Scrum is time-boxed to 15 minutes; detailed discussions can continue afterward
options.D: Run the meeting himself to keep it short

questions.explanation: The Scrum Master coaches the team to keep the Daily Scrum within its 15-minute time-box; follow-up discussions can happen afterward.

questions.question: True or False: Scrum is a complete methodology that prescribes detailed step-by-step techniques for building products.
questions.explanation: False. Scrum is a lightweight, intentionally incomplete framework; it does not prescribe detailed techniques.

questions.question: Developers complain they cannot finish anything because requirements keep changing mid-Sprint. Who should primarily help address this and protect the team's focus?

options.A: The Scrum Master, by coaching on Scrum and helping the team focus on the Sprint Goal
options.B: Management, by freezing all requirements permanently
options.C: The Product Owner, by never talking to the team during the Sprint
options.D: Nobody; this is normal and unavoidable

questions.explanation: The Scrum Master helps the team focus on the Sprint Goal and coaches the organization. The Sprint Goal stays fixed, and only the Product Owner can adjust product scope.

questions.question: Which of the following are true about the Sprint Goal? (Choose all that apply.)

options.A: It is the single objective for the Sprint
options.B: It is created during Sprint Planning
options.C: It can be abandoned freely by the Developers mid-Sprint
options.D: It provides flexibility regarding the exact work needed to achieve it

questions.explanation: The Sprint Goal is the single objective, crafted in Sprint Planning, and gives flexibility on the exact work. It is not abandoned freely mid-Sprint.

questions.question: The Scrum Team is cross-functional, which means:

options.A: Members can be moved to any other team at any time
options.B: The team has all the skills necessary to create value each Sprint
options.C: Everyone has exactly the same skills
options.D: The team depends on outside groups for most of its work

questions.explanation: Cross-functional means the team collectively has all skills needed to create value each Sprint without depending on others.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner may delegate Product Backlog management work to others but remains accountable.
questions.explanation: True. The Product Owner may delegate but remains accountable for Product Backlog management.

questions.question: When is an Increment considered "Done"?

options.A: When the Product Owner says so
options.B: When it meets the Definition of Done
options.D: When stakeholders approve it at the Review

questions.explanation: An Increment is "Done" when it meets the Definition of Done.

questions.question: Stakeholders try to give tasks directly to the Developers during the Sprint. What should the Scrum Master do?

options.A: Allow it to keep stakeholders happy
options.B: Coach the organization and stakeholders to route product decisions through the Product Owner and protect the team's focus
options.C: Tell the Developers to do whatever stakeholders ask

questions.explanation: The Scrum Master coaches the organization to channel product decisions through the Product Owner and protects the team's focus.

questions.question: True or False: The Sprint Backlog is fully fixed at Sprint Planning and cannot change during the Sprint.
questions.explanation: False. The Sprint Backlog is updated throughout the Sprint as the Developers learn more; it is a real-time picture of the plan.

questions.question: The purpose of the Sprint is to:

options.B: Provide a fixed-length period in which a usable Increment is produced, creating consistency toward the Product Goal

questions.explanation: The Sprint creates consistency: a fixed-length period that yields a usable Increment toward the Product Goal.

questions.question: Who attends the Sprint Review?

options.B: The Scrum Team and key stakeholders invited by the Product Owner
options.C: Only the Product Owner and stakeholders

questions.explanation: The Sprint Review is attended by the Scrum Team and key stakeholders invited by the Product Owner.

questions.question: A team has no Definition of Done. What is the best guidance from the Scrum Master?

options.A: Continue without one; it is optional
options.B: The Scrum Team must create a Definition of Done so the Increment is transparent
options.C: Wait for management to provide one
options.D: Let each Developer use their own personal definition

questions.explanation: A Definition of Done is essential for transparency; if none exists, the Scrum Team must create one.

questions.question: True or False: In Scrum 2020, the term "roles" was replaced with "accountabilities," and there is no separate "Development Team" sub-unit — only Developers within one Scrum Team.
questions.explanation: True. Scrum 2020 uses "accountabilities" rather than "roles," and the Developers are simply members of the one Scrum Team (no separate Development Team).

pspo.name: Professional Scrum Product Owner™ I
pspo.description: Product Owner – Wert, Product Backlog, Stakeholder und Product Goal (Scrum Guide 2020).

questions.question: The Product Owner is accountable for:

options.B: Maximizing the value of the product resulting from the work of the Scrum Team

questions.explanation: The Product Owner is accountable for maximizing the value of the product resulting from the work of the Scrum Team.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner can be a committee of several people who vote on backlog priorities.
questions.explanation: False. The Product Owner is one person, not a committee. They may represent the needs of many stakeholders, but decisions go through the one Product Owner.

questions.question: Which activities are part of Product Backlog management? (Choose all that apply.)

options.A: Developing and explicitly communicating the Product Goal
options.B: Creating and clearly communicating Product Backlog items

questions.explanation: Product Backlog management includes developing/communicating the Product Goal, creating and clearly communicating items, and ordering them. Implementing items is the Developers' work.

questions.question: According to the Scrum Guide 2020, a product is best described as:

options.B: A vehicle to deliver value, with a clear boundary, known stakeholders, and well-defined users or customers
options.C: A project with a fixed end date

questions.explanation: The Scrum Guide 2020 defines a product as a vehicle to deliver value with a clear boundary, known stakeholders, and well-defined users or customers.

questions.question: Who decides the order (priority) of the Product Backlog?
questions.explanation: The Product Owner orders the Product Backlog.

questions.question: The Product Goal is:

options.B: A long-term objective for the Scrum Team that resides in the Product Backlog
options.C: The same as the Sprint Goal

questions.explanation: The Product Goal is the long-term objective for the Scrum Team and lives in the Product Backlog (it is the Product Backlog's commitment).

questions.question: True or False: For the Product Owner to succeed, the entire organization must respect their decisions.
questions.explanation: True. The whole organization must respect the Product Owner's decisions for them to be effective.

questions.question: Several Scrum Teams work on the same product. According to the Scrum Guide, how many Product Owners and Product Backlogs should there be?

options.A: One Product Owner and one Product Backlog per team
options.B: One Product Owner and one Product Backlog for the product, shared by all the teams
options.C: One Product Owner for the product, but a separate Product Backlog per team
options.D: A committee of Product Owners sharing one Product Backlog

questions.explanation: When several Scrum Teams work on one product, they share one Product Owner, one Product Backlog, and one Product Goal (Scrum Guide 2020).

questions.question: When can a "Done" Increment be released to stakeholders?

options.B: Only at the very end of the Sprint
options.C: At any point during the Sprint, as soon as it adds value; the Sprint Review is not a gate to releasing value
options.D: Only after the Product Owner writes a formal release plan

questions.explanation: A usable Increment can be released whenever it adds value; the Scrum Guide states the Sprint Review should never be considered a gate to releasing value.

questions.question: What is the consequence if the Product Backlog and the other artifacts lack transparency?

options.A: Nothing significant; the team simply works faster
options.B: Decisions intended to optimize value and control risk are based on a flawed picture, which can diminish value and increase risk
options.C: The Scrum Master is required to cancel the Sprint
options.D: The Developers automatically become more productive

questions.explanation: Artifacts must be transparent. Decisions based on low-transparency artifacts can diminish value and increase risk (Scrum Guide 2020).

questions.question: Maximizing value means the Product Owner should:

options.A: Build as many features as possible
options.B: Order the Product Backlog so the most valuable work is done first
options.C: Always implement stakeholder requests in the order received
options.D: Maximize the number of items marked "Done"

questions.explanation: Maximizing value means ordering the backlog so the most valuable work is done first — not maximizing output.

questions.question: True or False: A good Product Owner often says "no" to keep the product focused and valuable.
questions.explanation: True. Saying "no" to low-value work is part of maximizing value and keeping the product focused.

questions.question: Which statement about the Product Backlog is correct?

options.A: It is a fixed contract agreed at the start
options.B: It is an emergent, ordered list of what is needed to improve the product, and it is never complete
options.D: It is owned by the Scrum Master

questions.explanation: The Product Backlog is an emergent, ordered list of what is needed to improve the product; it is never complete.

questions.question: The single source of work for the Scrum Team is:
questions.explanation: The Product Backlog is the single source of work undertaken by the Scrum Team.

questions.question: Stakeholders are most directly engaged by the Product Owner at which event?
questions.explanation: The Sprint Review is the key event where the Product Owner engages stakeholders to inspect the Increment and adapt the backlog.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner may delegate writing Product Backlog items to the Developers but remains accountable.
questions.explanation: True. Writing items can be delegated, but the Product Owner remains accountable.

questions.question: A key purpose of a product vision is to:

options.B: Provide a shared, longer-term direction that guides decisions and the Product Goal

questions.explanation: A product vision gives shared, longer-term direction that informs the Product Goal and decisions.

questions.question: The Product Owner wants to forecast a likely release date. Which approach fits Scrum best?

options.A: Commit to a fixed date and scope up front
options.B: Use empirical data (e.g., past throughput) and the ordered backlog to forecast, while accepting uncertainty
options.C: Ask management to set the date

questions.explanation: Forecasting in Scrum uses empirical data and the ordered backlog while accepting inherent uncertainty.

questions.question: How do Product Backlog items become ready enough to bring into a Sprint?

options.A: They are signed off by management
options.B: Through ongoing refinement, until they are sufficiently clear and small to be done within a Sprint

questions.explanation: Items become ready through ongoing refinement until they are clear and small enough for a Sprint.

questions.question: True or False: Maximizing the amount of work delivered is the same as maximizing value.
questions.explanation: False. Output is not the same as value; delivering more work does not necessarily deliver more value.

questions.question: Effective Product Backlog management includes ensuring the Product Backlog is:

options.D: Maintained solely by the Scrum Master

questions.explanation: The Product Owner ensures the Product Backlog is transparent, visible, and understood.

questions.question: A stakeholder demands a feature the Product Owner believes has low value. What is the best response?

options.A: Add it to the top of the backlog immediately
options.B: Seek to understand the underlying need, weigh value against alternatives, and order accordingly
options.C: Refuse to talk to the stakeholder

questions.explanation: The Product Owner seeks the underlying need and orders by value rather than reacting to pressure.

questions.question: True or False: Only the Product Owner can cancel a Sprint.
questions.explanation: True. Only the Product Owner can cancel a Sprint.

questions.question: Which is the best measure of progress for a value-focused Product Owner?

options.B: Working, valuable Increments and the outcomes they deliver

questions.explanation: Progress toward value is measured by working, valuable Increments and the outcomes they produce, not by output metrics.

questions.question: The Product Owner's relationship to the Sprint Goal is best described as:

options.A: They define it alone and impose it on the team
options.B: They collaborate with the Developers to craft it during Sprint Planning
options.C: They have no involvement in it

questions.explanation: The Product Owner collaborates with the Developers to craft the Sprint Goal during Sprint Planning.

questions.question: How many Product Goals can a Scrum Team actively pursue at the same time?

options.A: As many as the Product Backlog contains
options.B: One; the Scrum Team must fulfill (or abandon) the current Product Goal before taking on the next
options.D: Three, to match the three artifacts

questions.explanation: A Scrum Team focuses on one Product Goal at a time and must fulfill or abandon it before taking on the next.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Backlog may contain items beyond features, such as fixes, improvements, and knowledge-acquisition work.
questions.explanation: True. The backlog can include features, fixes, improvements, and knowledge-acquisition (e.g., research/spikes) work.

questions.question: The Product Owner orders the Product Backlog primarily to:

options.B: Optimize the value of the work the Developers perform
options.D: Reflect the order in which requests arrived

questions.explanation: Ordering optimizes the value of the work the Developers perform.

questions.question: During the Sprint, who decides how to change the Sprint Backlog's plan as new work is discovered?
questions.explanation: The Developers own the Sprint Backlog and decide how to adapt their plan; the Product Owner governs product scope/priority, not the "how."

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner should ensure the Developers understand Product Backlog items to the level needed.
questions.explanation: True. The Product Owner ensures items are understood to the level needed by the Developers.

questions.question: Which is a valid reason to cancel a Sprint?

options.B: The Sprint Goal becomes obsolete (e.g., the company changes direction)
options.C: A stakeholder is unhappy with progress
options.D: The Scrum Master is on vacation

questions.explanation: A Sprint may be cancelled when its Sprint Goal becomes obsolete; being behind schedule is not a valid reason.

questions.question: A product roadmap is best understood as:

options.B: A communication tool expressing intended direction that may change as learning occurs

questions.explanation: A roadmap communicates intended direction and is expected to change as the team learns.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner is accountable for the value and business outcomes of the product.
questions.explanation: True. The Product Owner is accountable for the product's value and business outcomes.

questions.question: Who is responsible for sizing (estimating) Product Backlog items?

options.C: The Developers who will perform the work

questions.explanation: The Developers who will do the work are responsible for sizing items; the Product Owner may influence them by helping them understand trade-offs.

questions.question: When stakeholders and the Product Owner disagree on priorities, the final decision rests with:
questions.explanation: The Product Owner makes the final prioritization decision.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner participates in the Sprint Retrospective as a member of the Scrum Team.
questions.explanation: True. The Retrospective involves the whole Scrum Team, including the Product Owner.

questions.question: The best way for a Product Owner to validate whether a product assumption is correct is to:

options.B: Deliver a valuable Increment and measure the actual outcome with users
options.D: Wait until the end of the project

questions.explanation: Assumptions are validated empirically by delivering a valuable Increment and measuring real outcomes with users.

questions.question: The Product Backlog item at the top of the backlog should generally be:

options.B: The most clearly defined and small enough to be brought into a Sprint

questions.explanation: Higher-ordered items are typically clearer and smaller, ready to be brought into a Sprint.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner must personally write every Product Backlog item.
questions.explanation: False. The Product Owner may delegate item writing while remaining accountable.

questions.question: Which statement about the relationship between the Product Goal and the Product Backlog is correct?

options.B: The Product Goal is in the Product Backlog, and the rest of the Product Backlog emerges to define what will fulfill the Product Goal
options.C: The Product Backlog is the commitment for the Sprint Goal
options.D: A Product Goal removes the need for a Product Backlog

questions.explanation: The Product Goal resides in the Product Backlog; the rest of the backlog emerges to define what will fulfill that goal (Scrum Guide 2020).

questions.question: Which best describes the Product Owner during Sprint Planning?

options.A: Absent; planning is for the Developers only
options.B: Present to clarify Product Backlog items and help craft a valuable Sprint Goal

questions.explanation: During Sprint Planning the Product Owner clarifies items and helps the team craft a valuable Sprint Goal.

questions.question: True or False: It is acceptable for the Product Owner to change the order of the Product Backlog at any time.
questions.explanation: True. The Product Owner may reorder the Product Backlog at any time to reflect changing value.

questions.question: A Product Owner is pressured to add scope mid-Sprint that endangers the Sprint Goal. The best response is to:

options.B: Discuss it with the Developers; if it threatens the Sprint Goal, defer it to a future Sprint — or, in extreme cases, cancel the Sprint

questions.explanation: The Product Owner discusses the change with the Developers; if it endangers the Sprint Goal, it should wait — cancellation is a last resort.

questions.question: The stakeholders the Product Owner represents primarily include:

options.B: Customers, users, and other stakeholders whose needs the product serves

questions.explanation: The Product Owner represents customers, users, and other stakeholders served by the product.

questions.question: True or False: In Scrum, value is best confirmed through working Increments delivered to users rather than through detailed up-front specification.
questions.explanation: True. Value is confirmed empirically through working Increments delivered to users, not through up-front specification alone.

questions.question: A "minimum viable product" approach helps a Product Owner primarily by:

options.A: Reducing the team to its minimum size
options.B: Delivering the smallest valuable slice to learn from real usage and reduce risk

questions.explanation: An MVP delivers the smallest valuable slice to enable learning from real usage and reduce risk.

questions.question: Which is the strongest indicator that a Product Owner is effective?

options.A: The Product Backlog is very large
options.B: The product is delivering increasing value to customers and the organization
options.C: Every stakeholder request has been implemented
options.D: The team's velocity is as high as possible

questions.explanation: Effectiveness shows in the product delivering increasing value — not in backlog size, request count, or maximized velocity.

questions.question: True or False: The Product Owner should ensure transparency of the Product Backlog so stakeholders understand what the team will work on next.
questions.explanation: True. Transparency of the Product Backlog lets stakeholders understand upcoming work.

questions.question: When forecasting and release planning, which Scrum principle applies most?

options.A: Defined process control with no change
options.B: Empiricism: inspect actual progress and adapt the forecast

questions.explanation: Forecasting and release planning rely on empiricism: inspect actual progress and adapt.

questions.question: The Product Owner's ultimate goal can best be summarized as:

options.B: Maximizing the value of the product for customers, users, and the organization
options.C: Producing as many Increments as possible

questions.explanation: The Product Owner's ultimate goal is to maximize the value of the product for customers, users, and the organization.
